creator: khm@sciops.net
text:
Greetings,

During a recent round of 'pkg update', pkg decided to install bash and bash-completion packages.  Attempting to remove them results in sadness:

# pkg remove bash
Checking integrity... done (0 conflicting)
Deinstallation has been requested for the following 29 packages (of 0 packages in the universe):

Installed packages to be REMOVED:
        ImageMagick-nox11: 6.9.9.28_2,1
        bash: 5.0.18_3
        bash-completion: 2.11,2
        cups: 2.3.3_1
        ffmpeg: 4.3.1_1,1
        ghostscript9-agpl-base: 9.52_8
        git: 2.28.0
        gnupg: 2.2.22
        gnutls: 3.6.14_1
        gpgme: 1.14.0
        ja-tex-ptex: 3.6_1
        mutt: 1.14.6
        p11-kit: 0.23.21
        p5-subversion: 1.14.0
        subversion: 1.14.0
        tex-aleph: 1.15.2.1.r.4_4
        tex-dvipdfmx: 20150315_1
        tex-dvipsk: 5.995_2
        tex-formats: 20150521_2
        tex-jadetex: 3.13_3
        tex-luatex: 0.80.0_20
        tex-xdvik: 22.87_4
        tex-xetex: 0.99992_36
        tex-xmltex: 1.9_2
        texlive-base: 20150521_54
        texlive-docs: 20150523
        texlive-full: 20150521
        texlive-texmf: 20150523_4
        weechat: 2.9

Number of packages to be removed: 29

The operation will free 4 GiB.

Proceed with deinstalling packages? [y/N]: 
EOF

I did not note any message in the pkg output indicating why so many packages suddenly require the presence of bash, a shell FreeBSD has worked fine without for some years.

I've investigated the ports entries for the above software and can find no changes introducing dependencies on bash.

I assume this is some oversight on my part, but it seems to me that someone enabled bash-completion support in some subset of this software which caused this.  

If this is not a bug, please let me know where I can read more about this decision.
